description: Trigger discrimination for the authorization matrix (this skill) vs the audit record (audit-log-architect), external API/event contracts (api-event-architect), and the entitlement axis (plan-entitlement-architect); plus the operator console that ENFORCES policy (admin-console-architect), the subordinate row-filter scope axis (intra-tenant-scope-architect), and bearer share-link capability for non-members (share-link-access-architect).

cases.prompt: Design roles and permissions so members can't export data, admins can't touch billing, and support can't see tenant data without an approved, expiring grant.
cases.reason: Who CAN do what, per role, with brokered support access — the authorization matrix.

cases.prompt: We need a tamper-evident record of every admin action, permission change, and data export for SOC 2.
cases.reason: Recording what happened is the audit trail; no permission rules are being designed.

cases.prompt: Define the webhook events and payload schemas partner integrations subscribe to when records change.
cases.reason: External event contracts for integrations, not internal audit records and not permission design.

cases.prompt: Decide whether API access is included in the Pro plan and how the limit differs on Enterprise.
cases.reason: "Does this plan include X" is the entitlement axis; "can this role do X" is the authorization axis.

cases.prompt: Design the internal admin console: least-privilege operator tiers, audited cross-tenant access, marked time-boxed impersonation, break-glass elevation, and a control-plane for retry/failover/data-repair.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — the console operators ACT through (tiers, impersonation mechanics, break-glass, control-plane actions) ENFORCES policy; this skill authors the roles×permissions and impersonation RULES it enforces.
cases.reason: The operator action surface that enforces policy is admin-console-architect. This skill authors the authorization policy (roles × permissions, support/impersonation rules) that console enforces; it is not the console.

cases.prompt: Inside a tenant, limit each user to a subset of that tenant's rows by their site/region — design the per-user scope grants and the row-filter predicate on every scoped table.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — a propagated row-FILTER dimension, not a permission: the same role can carry different scopes.
cases.reason: A subordinate per-user row-filter scope axis is intra-tenant-scope-architect. This skill designs what a ROLE can do (permissions × resources); a scope is a filter dimension a role carries, not a permission.

cases.prompt: Let anyone with a link open a specific resource without an account — unguessable, expiring, revocable, optionally password-protected, granting only view on that one resource.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — an unguessable expiring bearer token scoping NON-members to one resource is a capability model, not identity-based member RBAC.
cases.reason: Anyone-with-the-link guest access via a bearer capability is share-link-access-architect. This skill designs identity-based member roles × permissions; a share link grants non-members access without a role.
